chief_complaint: An 80-year-old male patient presents with severe gastrointestinal bleeding.
history: The patient has a history of nonvalvular atrial fibrillation, coronary heart disease, and is a homozygous mutation carrier of the ABCB1 allele (rs1045642 C > T, rs1128503 C > T, rs2032582 G > T). He was developed severe gastrointestinal bleeding during administration of oral rivaroxaban combined with aspirin.
exam: Vital signs: tachycardia, hypotension. Physical examination: abdominal tenderness, melena.
labs: ECG: atrial fibrillation. Bloods: anemia, low hemoglobin. Imaging: not mentioned. Other tests: genetic testing for ABCB1 allele.
correct_diagnosis: Severe gastrointestinal bleeding due to rivaroxaban and aspirin interaction, exacerbated by ABCB1 gene polymorphism.
differentials: Severe gastrointestinal bleeding due to rivaroxaban and aspirin interaction, exacerbated by ABCB1 gene polymorphism.
correct_ranking: Severe gastrointestinal bleeding due to rivaroxaban and aspirin interaction, exacerbated by ABCB1 gene polymorphism.
